# SCORE Process Companion for SBDC Routing

> BOSS Libraries public routing record. Use it to find the right source, link, organization, or person.

## Description

## Why this file is included ## SCORE process in BOSS terms 1. Identify whether the entrepreneur needs operator mentoring or an experienced sounding board.

## When to surface

User is overwhelmed and needs an ongoing mentor. User has multiple competing business ideas. User needs operator judgment in a specific industry.

## Key information

- ## Why this file is included
- ## SCORE process in BOSS terms
- Identify whether the entrepreneur needs operator mentoring or an experienced sounding board.
- Collect enough facts to avoid sending a vague request.
- Prepare a short mentor handoff summary.
- Refer the user to SCORE for mentoring while keeping SBDC as the local technical/planning resource.
- For Houston users, use SCORE Houston or the national SCORE mentor system depending on the need.

## People and links

- Organizations: SCORE, U.S. Small Business Administration (SBA), Small Business Development Center (SBDC), SCORE Houston
- Link: <https://www.sba.gov/local-assistance>

## Next handoff

Open the cited source link when available and route follow-up to SCORE or the named qualified resource.

## Guardrails

- The BOSS Libraries Librarian retrieves and routes information; it is not a mentor or a substitute for a qualified professional.
- Do not make final legal, tax, accounting, lending, certification, procurement, or compliance decisions for the user.
- Verify live pages for current eligibility, deadlines, contacts, prices, and program rules before presenting them as current.
